code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a fluent, natural‑language sentence.
    Predicates are turned into readable phrases; triples that share the
    same subject are merged into one sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    import re

    # Mapping of predicates to natural language phrases.
    # The placeholder {obj} will be replaced by the object value.
    phrase_map = {
        "alternativeName": "also known as {obj}",
        "bodyStyle": "with body style {obj}",
        "engine": "powered by {obj}",
        "manufacturer": "manufactured by {obj}",
        "relatedMeanOfTransportation": "related to {obj}",
        "transmission": "with {obj} transmission",
        "wheelbase": "with a wheelbase of {obj}",
        "builder": "built by {obj}",
        "completionDate": "completed on {obj}",
        "length": "measuring {obj} in length",
        "powerType": "using {obj} power",
        "shipClass": "classified as {obj}",
        "shipDisplacement": "displacing {obj}",
        "shipLaunch": "launched on {obj}",
        "shipOrdered": "ordered on {obj}",
        "shipPower": "with {obj}",
        "topSpeed": "with a top speed of {obj}",
        "location": "located in {obj}",
        "christeningDate": "christened on {obj}",
        "maidenVoyage": "maiden voyage on {obj}",
        "owner": "owned by {obj}",
        "shipBeam": "with a beam of {obj}",
        "shipInService": "in service since {obj}",
        "status": "{obj}",
        "activeYearsStartDate": "active since {obj}",
        "shipLaidDown": "laid down on {obj}",
        "buildDate": "built {obj}",
        "cylinderCount": "with {obj} cylinders",
        "totalProduction": "total production of {obj} units",
        "countryOrigin": "originating from {obj}",
        "diameter": "with a diameter of {obj}",
        "failedLaunches": "with {obj} failed launch(es)",
        "rocketStages": "with {obj} rocket stage(s)",
        "totalLaunches": "with {obj} total launch(es)",
        "assembly": "assembled in {obj}",
        "class": "a {obj}",
        "designer": "designed by {obj}",
        "modelYears": "model year {obj}",
        "country": "of {obj}",
        "foundationPlace": "founded in {obj}",
        "foundedBy": "founded by {obj}",
        "designCompany": "designed by {obj}",
        "productionStartYear": "production started {obj}",
        "width": "with a width of {obj}",
        "layout": "with {obj} layout",
        "parentCompany": "subsidiary of {obj}",
        "operator": "operated by {obj}",
        "product": "produces {obj}",
        "city": "based in {obj}",
        "successor": "succeeded by {obj}",
        "fate": "fate was {obj}",
        "keyPerson": "key person {obj}",
        "subsidiary": "subsidiary {obj}",
        "comparable": "comparable to {obj}",
        "finalFlight": "final flight on {obj}",
        "function": "serves as {obj}",
        "launchSite": "launched from {obj}",
        "maidenFlight": "maiden flight on {obj}",
        "capital": "capital {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym {obj}",
        "leader": "led by {obj}",
        "partialFailures": "with {obj} partial failure(s)",
        "site": "at {obj}",
        "headquarter": "headquartered in {obj}",
        "associatedRocket": "associated with {obj}",
        "saint": "saint {obj}",
        "employer": "employer {obj}",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group {obj}",
        "language": "language {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title {obj}",
        "anthem": "anthem {obj}",
        "productionEndYear": "production ended {obj}",
        "division": "division {obj}",
        "shipDraft": "with a draft of {obj}",
        "modelStartYear": "model started {obj}",
        "areaTotal": "covering {obj} km²",
        "isPartOf": "part of {obj}",
        "extinctionDate": "extinct on {obj}",
    }

    def predicate_phrase(p: str, obj: str) -> str:
        # Use explicit mapping if available
        if p in phrase_map:
            return phrase_map[p].format(obj=obj)
        # Fallback: split camelCase / PascalCase and prepend a generic verb
        words = re.sub(r'([a-z])([A-Z])', r'\1 \2', p).lower()
        return f"has {words} {obj}"

    # Helper to clean object strings (remove quotes and surrounding whitespace, preserve units)
    def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip()
        # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
        if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
            o = o[1:-1]
        # Clean up internal quotes
        o = o.replace('"', '')
        # Convert numeric values for nicer phrasing
        try:
            # Detect pure numeric strings (int or float)
            if o.replace('.', '', 1).isdigit():
                num = float(o)
                # If the number is an integer after conversion, drop the decimal part
                if num.is_integer():
                    o = str(int(num))
                else:
                    # Keep reasonable decimal precision
                    o = str(round(num, 2)).rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
        except Exception:
            pass
        return o

    # Group triples by subject
    from collections import defaultdict
    grouped = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        # clean the object before storing
        cleaned = clean_obj(t.object)
        grouped[t.subject].append(Triple(t.subject, t.predicate, cleaned))

    # Build clauses for each subject
    clause_parts = []
    for subj, items in grouped.items():
        # Ensure a deterministic order of predicates for reproducibility
        items = sorted(items, key=lambda tr: tr.predicate)
        # Build predicate phrases for this subject
        preds = [predicate_phrase(t.predicate, t.object) for t in items]
        if len(preds) == 1:
            body = preds[0]
        elif len(preds) == 2:
            body = " and ".join(preds)
        else:
            body = ", ".join(preds[:-1]) + ", and " + preds[-1]
        clause_parts.append(f"{subj} {body}")

    # Join all subject clauses into a single sentence
    if not clause_parts:
        return ""
    if len(clause_parts) == 1:
        sentence = clause_parts[0] + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clause_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + clause_parts[-1] + "."
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

  # Helper to clean object strings (remove parentheses, extra quotes and surrounding whitespace)
      def clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
          o = o.strip()
          # Remove surrounding double quotes if present
          if o.startswith('"') and o.endswith('"'):
              o = o[1:-1]
          # Remove content in parentheses (e.g., units) and trim again
          if '(' in o:
              o = o.split('(')[0].strip()
          # Convert numeric values like \"120.0\" to \"120\" for nicer phrasing
          try:
              # Detect pure numeric strings (int or float)
              if o.replace('.', '', 1).isdigit():
                  num = float(o)
                  # If the number is an integer after conversion, drop the decimal part
                  if num.is_integer():
                      o = str(int(num))
                  else:
                      # Keep reasonable decimal precision
                      o = str(round(num, 2)).rstrip('0').rstrip('.')
          except Exception:
              pass
          return o
